title: Stelechocarpus schefferi
intro: Stelechocarpus schefferi, also known as the velvet apple or velvet fruit, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and diarrhea. The bioactive constituents of Stelechocarpus schefferi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Stelechocarpus schefferi is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, with the most common herbal preparations being teas and infusions made from the leaves and fruit of the plant.
taxonomy: Stelechocarpus schefferi is classified within the family Annonaceae. This family is a part of the order Magnoliales, and the class Magnoliopsida. The order Magnoliales is a part of the subclass Magnoliidae, which is a subgroup of the division Magnoliophyta. The division Magnoliophyta is further classified within the kingdom Plantae. The kingdom Plantae is a part of the domain Eukaryota.
uses: Stelechocarpus schefferi, also known as the Ivory Nut or the mountain walnut, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
